title: ODI Matrix — NAICS 221118 Other Electric Power Generation (Standalone BESS / IPP)

sections.title: Step 1 — Gather error statements (merge Step 06 market + Step 10 product-related)
sections.content:
- 6 market-level needs (from FIT Step 09 references to Step 06 needs #3, #11, #13, #18, #21, #22) → `product_related=false`, full job-step context preserved where named.
- 12 product-related needs lifted verbatim from `FIT_other_electric_power_generation_bess_ref.md` Step 10 → `product_related=true`, `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name=product_constraint`, `impact_category=reliability`.
- Total entries in scope for 221118: **18**

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction recalibration vs. incumbent
sections.content:
**Incumbent profile used for recalibration** (derived from FIT Step 11 + Product-Decomp VN section):

| Incumbent | Share | Mechanism | Top strengths | Top weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Fluence / Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan / Wärtsilä (OEM-led turnkey) | ~40% | Vertically integrated L2→L0 container + PCS + EMS | Deep BMS/thermal, factory-tested propagation containment, tight PCS↔BMS FCR response, scale €/kWh | Vendor lock-in / equipment-locked wrap, no PV heritage, cert via local subcontractor, change-order opacity |

**Recalibration rule:** satisfaction raised where incumbent strength directly addresses the need (thermal containment, RTE, CAPEX scale, BMS cycle mgmt); held or lowered where incumbent weakness aligns (lock-in, DE DSO cert, single-point wrap, supply-chain alt-qual).

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Scored ODIEntry table (recalibrated · deterministic opp score)
sections.content:
| # | ID | Error statement | Src | Imp | Sat₀ | Sat_recal | Opp | Under | Over |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | M1 | Minimize interface ambiguity between EPC contract scopes causing site delays | mkt | 8.5 | 3.5 | 4.5 | **12.5** | ✅ | |
| 2 | M2 | Minimize likelihood of missing FGW TR3/TR8 dossier elements blocking DSO acceptance | mkt | 8.5 | 3.5 | 3.5 | **13.5** | ✅ | |
| 3 | M3 | Minimize commissioning-recovery delays after a failed DSO acceptance test | mkt | 8.0 | 4.5 | 4.5 | **11.5** | ✅ | |
| 4 | M4 | Minimize change-order opacity under single-vendor OEM equipment lock-in | mkt | 7.5 | 4.5 | 4.0 | **11.0** | ✅ | |
| 5 | M5 | Minimize multi-vendor warranty-chase time across battery / PCS / grid / civil | mkt | 7.5 | 4.5 | 5.5 | 9.5 | | |
| 6 | M6 | Minimize SLA-evidence gap from BMS telemetry for availability and throughput reporting | mkt | 7.0 | 5.0 | 6.0 | 8.0 | | |
| 7 | PR1 | Minimize propagating thermal-runaway in an LFP cell triggering Feuerwehr-attended container write-off | prod | 9.5 | 5.0 | 6.5 | **12.5** | | |
| 8 | PR2 | Minimize NOVEC 1230 / aerosol false-discharge causing unplanned container decommissioning | prod | 6.5 | 5.5 | 5.5 | 7.5 | | |
| 9 | PR3 | Minimize Brandschutzkonzept rework from >3 MWh cluster separation walls missed in first Feuerwehr review | prod | 7.5 | 4.0 | 4.0 | **11.0** | ✅ | |
| 10 | PR4 | Minimize LFP container footprint (40–80 m²/MWh) disqualifying otherwise viable sites | prod | 6.0 | 5.0 | 5.0 | 7.0 | | |
| 11 | PR5 | Minimize LFP-cell single-country tariff / CRMA exposure exceeding 70% of project CAPEX | prod | 7.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 10.0 | | |
| 12 | PR6 | Minimize time to re-qualify an alternate LFP cell supplier (FAT, UN38.3, IEC 62619) after disruption | prod | 6.5 | 3.5 | 3.5 | 9.5 | | |
| 13 | PR7 | Minimize LFP capacity derating reducing dispatch revenue at ambient <−10 °C or >+40 °C beyond HVAC | prod | 7.0 | 5.5 | 6.5 | 7.5 | | |
| 14 | PR8 | Minimize HVAC parasitic load exceeding 3% of throughput across the annual temperature envelope | prod | 6.0 | 5.0 | 6.0 | 6.0 | | |
| 15 | PR9 | Minimize likelihood AC-AC RTE falls below 85% on a 10-year degradation curve | prod | 8.0 | 5.5 | 6.5 | 9.5 | | |
| 16 | PR10 | Minimize LFP capacity fade (SoH <80% before Year 10) shortening revenue life vs. PPA term | prod | 8.5 | 5.0 | 6.0 | **11.0** | | |
| 17 | PR11 | Minimize installed €/kWh exceeding the 250–400 band that gates the arbitrage / peak-shaving case | prod | 8.0 | 4.5 | 5.5 | 10.5 | | |
| 18 | PR12 | Minimize LFP cell-price volatility (±20% YoY) invalidating the EPC offer between FID and procurement lock | prod | 7.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 10.0 | | |

**Formula spot-check (Q1):** M2 → 8.5 + max(8.5−3.5,0) = 13.5 ✅ · PR1 → 9.5 + max(9.5−6.5,0) = 12.5 ✅ · PR8 → 6.0 + max(6.0−6.0,0) = 6.0 ✅ · M5 → 7.5 + max(7.5−5.5,0) = 9.5 ✅ · PR11 → 8.0 + max(8.0−5.5,0) = 10.5 ✅

**Recalibration rationale (selected):**
- PR1, PR7, PR10 → raised: OEM vertical integration (Tesla / Sungrow) = factory-tested thermal-propagation containment + deeper BMS cycle mgmt → incumbent delivers above initial LLM estimate.
- PR9 → raised: OEM PCS-BMS native integration tighter than arm's-length PCS procurement.
- PR11 → raised: OEM scale economics beat EPC-integrator BoM cost for pure containers.
- M4 → lowered: OEM wrap is specifically equipment-locked; change-order opacity worse than original estimate.
- M1, M2, M3, PR3, PR5, PR6, PR12 → held: DSO/DE-cert/Brandschutz/supply-alt-qual not meaningfully addressed by any incumbent profile.

---

### Top 5 opportunities (by opp score, descending)

| Rank | Opp | Statement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 13.5 | Minimize likelihood of missing FGW TR3/TR8 dossier elements blocking DSO acceptance |
| 2 | 12.5 | Minimize interface ambiguity between EPC contract scopes causing site delays |
| 3 | 12.5 | Minimize propagating thermal-runaway in an LFP cell triggering Feuerwehr-attended container write-off |
| 4 | 11.5 | Minimize commissioning-recovery delays after a failed DSO acceptance test |
| 5 | 11.0 | Minimize change-order opacity under single-vendor OEM equipment lock-in |

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-check vs. Feature-Market Fit (Step 09 in FIT doc)
sections.content:
| Check | ODI avg | Feat-Fit avg | Finding |
|---|---|---|---|
| 221118 | **9.89** | **6.50** (Step 09 mean of 6 tech features: 6.11, 6.22, 6.11, 6.89, 8.11, 5.56) | No flag. ODI in 8–12 band, Feat-Fit in 4–7 band → no "high opportunity / low fit" (>12 vs <4) or "low opportunity / high fit" (<8 vs >7) trip. Step 09 strongest feature (Grid-Code Cert, 8.11) directly maps to the top-ranked ODI opportunity (M2 TR8 dossier gap, 13.5) — coherent. |

**ODI ↔ Step 11 vendor-fit alignment:**
- Top ODI opp M2 (TR3/TR8 dossier) ↔ Vendor Grid-Code Cert parity vs. Fluence (native DE PV-EPC practice). **Mein Solar can convert this opportunity without a capability gap.**
- M1, M4 (interface ambiguity, change-order opacity) ↔ Vendor Single-Source EPC (parity, gap ≤1 with lower lock-in). **Convertible with lock-in-free wrap positioning.**
- PR1 (thermal-runaway) ↔ Vendor Containerized Integration **inferior** (6 vs 9 vs Tesla/Sungrow). **Opp exists but vendor weaker than OEM incumbent — do not position on thermal-propagation claims.**

---
